Chicago's Playable Pinball Museum

From the wooden bagatelle tables of the 1800s to today's cutting-edge machines — experience the full history of pinball, hands-on.

Now open in The Loop

In the lower level of Block 37, 108 N State St, Chicago

Plan Your VisitView Collection

A curated selection from our growing collection of playable pinball machines spanning nearly a century of innovation.

View Full Collection →

Our Mission

Connecting everyone with the history, art, science, and technology of pinball.

Chicago has been the center of the pinball industry since the 1930s. The Flip celebrates this legacy through hands-on exhibits, restored machines, and educational programs that inspire the next generation of pinball enthusiasts.

Learn Our Story →

What's Happening

Upcoming Events

Open houses, tours, repair days, and more. Come play with us.

Jun19

Open House Night

Friday · 3:22 PM · 108 N. State St., Suite 015

Join us for an evening of free play on all our restored machines. All skill levels welcome!

Recurring
Jun23

A Brief History of Fun Tour

Tuesday · 3:22 PM · 108 N. State St., Suite 015

A 90-minute guided tour through pinball history, from ancient Rome to modern machines.

Jun26

Saturday Repair Day

Friday · 3:22 PM · 108 N. State St., Suite 015

Learn the art of pinball machine repair and restoration with our volunteer technicians.

Recurring
